tests.intent: Every database response audio item is exactly the reviewed five-field integer/string tuple with bounded volume and separation and no client-authored gameplay fields.
Transitions are ordered by logical tic, event class, source, target and authoritative event ordinal, then receive dense zero-based per-tic audio ordinals.
Map start, weapon, pickup, world machine, monster wake/pain/death, barrel, and player-pain scenarios emit the exact reviewed asset timeline.
A database transition without a relational audio definition emits nothing and the browser does not infer sound from controls, frame pixels, mode, or local state.
Event-to-asset mappings are relational, resolve seeded sound/music assets, and do not use hard-coded event CASE dispatch or E1M1 object identifiers.
A new E1M1 map emits one D_E1M1 music tuple at tic zero, uses its seeded browser-playable blob and does not restart it on ordinary STEP responses.
Database-authored volume and separation are exact bounded tuple values; client conversion only maps them to gain and pan and never recomputes spatial gameplay.
A repeated equal tic/ordinal tuple fails closed before playback, including an exact response retry delivered twice to the presentation layer.
Any lower tic or non-increasing ordinal relative to the accepted cursor fails closed rather than replaying, sorting, dropping, or guessing.
Wrong tuple arity, names, numeric types, negative indices, and out-of-range mix fields fail before cache access or AudioContext scheduling.
Every browser audio byte request is an application/json POST to DOOM_API.GET_ASSET with only p_asset_name; no table, blob, WAD, CDN, inferred, or direct asset URL is requested.
Concurrent and later occurrences of one asset share one GET_ASSET promise and one decoded AudioBuffer while distinct names remain separate cache keys.
GET_ASSET base64 BLOB bytes and media type are decoded with browser APIs and sound/music assets remain reproducible database-seeded payloads.
Events may fetch and decode before consent but no source starts until an explicit click resumes AudioContext; queued events then schedule exactly once.
Audio failure, latency, disabled audio, and scheduling time cannot mutate commands, session state, tic progression, event tuples, replay hashes, or gameplay decisions.
Pinned Chromium observes one resumed context and exact scheduled gain/pan order after a real user gesture with strict console, page-error, and request-failure guards.
Browser interception proves cache misses use only the approved AutoREST operation and repeated event assets cause no additional network request.
Audio rows are session-scoped inside the owning tic transaction, duplicate database keys fail, rollback removes them, and the emitter never commits autonomously.
Database and client source audits reject evaluator coupling, fixtures, embedded bytes, event inference, direct base-table HTTP, dynamic SQL, host timing gameplay, and transaction ownership.
Every isolated database, cache, ordering, transport, decoding, gesture, scheduling, and presentation-only defect is killed by one documented focused witness.
